violations.text: The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel/dishwasher thermometer (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Establishment to use dishwasher to wash ONLY. All equipment and utensils to be sanitized in the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

The gaskets of the hot-holding units were observed with frayed gaskets. Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
The faucet at the 3-bay sink was observed with a leak at the faucet. A plumbing system shall be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Deli meat was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. All foods prepped/cooked on site must be used or disposed of within 7 days. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH CFP at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Person in charge given CFSM application at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers in the walk-in cooler has an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025

One of the hot water faucets at the 3-bay sink was observed to be off. Hot water generation and distribution systems shall be sufficient to meet the peak hot water demands throughout the FOOD ESTABLISHMENT. Person in charge requested a maintenance service at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers of the walk-in refrigerator have an accumulation of dust. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024
The faucet of the 3-bay sink was observed to be in poor repair (leaking). A plumbing system shall be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

The walk-in refrigerator was observed to be in poor repair (maintaining ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit). Shelf stable items (salsa) was observed to be 47 degrees Fahrenheit and held in the cooler overnight. The time/temperature controlled foods (yogurt and cheeses) were observed to be 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Per PIC, foods were delivered under an hour prior to beginning of inspection and moved to different refrigeration. A service call was made at the time of inspection. Unit to not be used until it can be replaced/repaired/serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers of the walk-in refrigerator have an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024
The faucet of the 3-bay sink was observed to be in poor repair (leaking). A plumbing system shall be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified with RIDOH Center for Food Protection in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. An application was left with PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quatenary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers in the walk-in cooler has an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024
The faucet at the 3-bay sink was observed to be leaking. The plumbing system shall be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024
Boxes of vegetables were observed with an accumulation of ice on top of them in the walk-in freezer. Contamination must be prevented during storage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

The can opener, walk-in freezer, and oven door located in the kitchen area are not in good repair. The can opener blade is rusted, the walk-in freezer has ice accumulating on the floor and food, and the door on the oven won't open. Equipment must be repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

Chicken patties and hamburgers were observed held at 97-104 degrees Fahrenheit in the hot cabinet. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken patties and hamburgers were reheated to 135-148 during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023
Boxes of vegetables were observed with an accumulation of ice on top of them in the walk-in freezer. Contamination must be prevented during storage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

The can opener, walk-in freezer, and oven door located in the kitchen area are not in good repair. The can opener blade is rusted, the walk-in freezer has ice accumulating on the floor and food, and the door on the oven won't open. Equipment must be repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023
